key_insight: The paper shows that strong zero-shot reconstruction of dynamical systems can be achieved with a minimal, interpretable two-parameter model that linearly blends the current latent state with the nearest in-context neighbor and its successor.
engineering_relevance: This suggests foundation-model-style DS forecasters can be drastically simplified for more efficient, explainable, and analytically tractable deployment in scientific computing.
method: Iteratively reduce the prior SOTA DynaMix architecture to a minimal form called DynaBase.
Use a linear interpolation rule between the current latent state, nearest context neighbor, and that neighbor’s temporal successor.
Derive closed-form one-step prediction MSE solutions and a 1-parameter family of dynamics spanning context-parroting to chaotic bounded behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports competitive zero-shot reconstruction across both chaotic and cyclic dynamical systems, with parameter counts orders of magnitude below other foundation models.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how nearest in-context neighbors are defined and on whether the simplified form generalizes equally well beyond the evaluated dynamical-system benchmarks.
paper_title: A Minimal Interpretable Architecture for Zero-Shot Reconstruction of Dynamical Systems
